2003 Honda Pilot Overview

2003 Honda Pilot Pricing

2003 Honda Pilot pricing starts at $2,925 for the Pilot LX Sport Utility 4D, which had a starting MSRP of $27,360 when new. The range-topping 2003 Pilot EX-L Sport Utility 4D starts at $3,225 today, originally priced from $30,980.

Original MSRP
KBB Fair Purchase Price (national avg.)
LX Sport Utility 4D
$27,360
$2,925
EX Sport Utility 4D
$29,730
$3,475
EX-L Sport Utility 4D
$30,980
$3,225

The Kelley Blue Book Fair Purchase Price for any individual used vehicle can vary greatly according to mileage, condition, location, and other factors. The prices here reflect what buyers are currently paying for used 2003 Honda Pilot models in typical condition when purchasing from a dealership. These prices are updated weekly.

Pilot Consumer Sentiment

Owners give this generation Honda Pilot (2003-2008) a 4.7 out of 5 rating, which is higher than most, and 94% recommend it. These figures are based on 994 consumer reviews, like these:

What Owners are Saying About the 2003 Honda Pilot

"Quality"

"Seats are a little rough. Along with the seat belt placement. But the vehicle is very strong and does well in the snow."

"The best purchase I've ever made in my life!!!"

"I currently have 199,800 miles (and counting) on my 4WD 2005 Pilot. I purchased it in '08 with 29k miles on it. When I drove it home my neighbor said I'd NEVER have a problem with it. He was telling the truth!!!! The only way this SUV is leaving my possession is when I purchase its smaller counterpart (the CR-V) at which point I'll gift my Pilot to one of my (now grown) children. It's safe, durable, uber reliable and strong enough to uproot a tree (I did in the 1st month that I owned it). I have the EX-L and I love the front seat warmers, sunroof and leather seats. Matter of-fact, those will have to be a must in my CR-V. This car is so durable, I even love how the paint has held up. I really do believe this engine will last another 200K miles. I look forward to many more oil changes and life memories with grandchildren learning to drive this vehicle. Thanks Honda! Due to this purchase you've got a family of customers for life!"

"250k; aiming for 400k"

"I've owned the vehicle since it was new and it has been a fantastic vehicle. While others may not like the fuel economy, it is an appropriate gas efficiency for the utility it provides. I get roughly 18 in the winter (more city driving) and up to 22 in the summer (more highway driving), with AWD too. With gas prices at $3.40 a gallon, the total cost of ownership for 14 years and 250k can be broken down as follows: 10.6 cents/mile for Maintenance and Repairs (incl. 2 timing belt services), 19 cents/mile for gas, 12.5 cents/mile depreciation, 8 cents/mile insurance; total of 50 cents/mile. In comparison to a newer vehicle, I still can't find an equivalent value for a vehicle that I don't have to worry about and that is honest in what it is (I'm car touchscreen/gimic averse)."

Overall 2003 Honda Pilot Quality

User sentiment suggests the overall quality of the 2003 Honda Pilot is above average for its class. Owners rated the 2003 Honda Pilot quality a 4.8 out of 5.

2003 Honda Pilot Reliability

The 2003 Honda Pilot receives an average consumer rating of 4.7 overall and a 4.8 for reliability, which means owners consider its reliability to be above average.

Favorite 2003 Pilot Features

According to drivers of the 2003 Pilot, here are some of its best features:

  • Powerful. Roomy.
  • Pick up, reliability, comfortable to ride & drive
  • Reliable, honest
  • Smooth, powerful engine. Excellent dash controls.
  • Solid and sure on the road, a pleasure to drive!

2003 Pilot Safety

NHTSA Safety Rating

According to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), the 2003 Honda Pilot front-side driver crash test rating is 5 out of 5 stars. The front-side passenger crash test rating is 5 out of 5.

2003 Honda Pilot Safety Features

  • Child Door Locks
  • Child Seat Anchors
  • Driver Airbag
  • Passenger Airbag
  • Traction Control

John
2/18/2026
Like a Timex watch
Pros

Cargo room and engine dependability.

Cons

Aftermarket stereos are not compatible with the stock subwoofer.

As long as the regular maintenance is being done, especially the fluid replacements, this SUV will take a licking and keep on ticking. Decent gas mileage for its size. Can carry 8 passengers, or a sheet of plywood instead. Can tow a loaded utility trailer or a medium sized boat. Come snow or mud, soccer games or camping trips, this SUV always keeps time.

Best car I’ve owned. And I’m 75.

if regular check ups, oil, changes, etc. have been done, it lasts for God knows how long. I expect 250,000 or 300,000 miles.

Dria
2/17/2023
Honda Pilot 1st generation
Pros

Lots of room. Just keeps on running..

Cons

Shocks and struts. VTM-4 Light came on alot. Light

Most dependable and low maintenance vehicle I've owned. Still running after 200,000 plus miles. Great engine. Good gas mileage. Lots of room. Used my Honda for numerous camping trips. And moving furniture, bricks, fire wood, industrial size paints, you name it. Not to mention every day to and from work (two jobs.) One is a delivery job (using my vehicle) plus driving kids too and from soccer practices and games. To say the least my Honda really never stopped. I am finally getting rid of my Pilot because it needs back brakes, shocks, and struts plus its getting up there in miles. So I decided to sell it.

Justagirlsop
4/13/2022
Honda Impresses me again! -2003 Honda Pilot-
Pros

Fun to drive~ Watch your speed tho ~ speed kills

Cons

Uses more gasoline than I would like. That is it.

This 2003 Honda Pilot EX-L Is a 3.5 liter 4 wd with tow package. Gotz get up and Go! Drives great after replacing struts. Be prepared to replace front end or well I did, Just sayin’ . Watch your tire pressure; keep your tires psi right dont wait until yours tires are practically flat!. Also don’t let dealers charge you $150 for your radio code. Google it and do it for free with your radios serial number.

Difficult owner maintainability

Enjoy the car. Basically trouble free. I drive very conservative .I do not like how thirsty it is. 17mpg-ish. Do not that the V6 has a timing BELT. Not a chain. Transverse engine makes this Belt maintenance difficult.

Values above are based on "Good" condition vehicles. Estimated Trade-In Values range from about $850 to $1,800 depending on vehicle style. Estimated Private Party Values range from about $2,450 to $2,975 depending on vehicle style. Values and prices are based on national averages and typical mileage for this model, but they can vary greatly according to mileage, condition, location, and other factors. Valid through 6/15/2026.
Trade-In Value
Trade-In Value is the amount you can expect to receive this week when trading in your vehicle at a dealership.
Private Party Value
Private Party Value is the fair price you can expect when selling your car to an individual, not to a dealer.
Fair Purchase Price
Fair Purchase Price shows what others in your area are actually paying for this vehicle.

FAQs

Is 2003 a good year for a Honda Pilot?

Owners of the vehicle give it 4.7 out of 5 stars. To find out if the 2003 Pilot is the right car for you, check out the pros and cons, trims, specs and options at Kelley Blue Book.

Is the Honda Pilot 2003 a good SUV?

Owners of the vehicle give it 4.7 out of 5 stars. To find out if the 2003 Pilot is the right car for you, check out the pros and cons, trims, specs and options at Kelley Blue Book.
